---
title: "IPCC Tier 2 Livestock GHG Uncertainty Calculator"
subtitle: "Pre-Flight Questionnaire — GMH Uncertainty Translator"
author: "CIAT / CGIAR Alliance — ClimateActionNetZero"
date: "May 2026"
output:
  pdf_document:
    toc: false
    number_sections: false
    latex_engine: pdflatex
    keep_tex: false
  word_document:
    toc: false
header-includes:
  - \usepackage{booktabs}
  - \usepackage{longtable}
  - \usepackage{array}
  - \usepackage{xcolor}
  - \usepackage{fancyhdr}
  - \usepackage{graphicx}
  - \usepackage{hyperref}
  - \usepackage{setspace}
  - \usepackage{caption}
  - \usepackage{tabularx}
  - \definecolor{cgiargreen}{RGB}{45,106,79}
  - \definecolor{lightgreen}{RGB}{216,243,220}
  - \definecolor{darkgrey}{RGB}{60,60,60}
  - \definecolor{amber}{RGB}{180,100,0}
  - \setlength{\headheight}{14pt}
  - \pagestyle{fancy}
  - \fancyhf{}
  - \fancyhead[L]{\textcolor{cgiargreen}{\small IPCC Tier 2 Livestock GHG Uncertainty Calculator}}
  - \fancyhead[R]{\textcolor{darkgrey}{\small Pre-Flight Questionnaire}}
  - \fancyfoot[C]{\textcolor{darkgrey}{\small \thepage}}
  - \renewcommand{\headrulewidth}{0.4pt}
  - \renewcommand{\footrulewidth}{0pt}
  - \setlength{\parskip}{6pt}
  - \captionsetup{font=small,labelfont=bf}
  - \hypersetup{colorlinks=true, urlcolor=cgiargreen, linkcolor=cgiargreen}
geometry: "top=3cm, bottom=2.5cm, left=2.5cm, right=2.5cm"
fontsize: 11pt
linestretch: 1.2
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
```

\vspace{0.4cm}

\begin{center}
\textcolor{cgiargreen}{\rule{14cm}{1.5pt}}\\[6pt]
\textcolor{cgiargreen}{\large\textbf{Version 1.0 — May 2026}}\\[4pt]
\textcolor{darkgrey}{Fill this out before opening the Translator}\\[4pt]
\textcolor{cgiargreen}{\rule{14cm}{1.5pt}}
\end{center}

\vspace{0.4cm}

\noindent Fill this out \textbf{before} opening the Translator on \href{https://claude.ai/project/019e4594-d13d-761e-a6fb-71e242eb9804}{claude.ai}. When you start the chat, paste this whole filled page as your first message — Claude will use it to skip the warm-up questions and go straight to inspecting your data.

\noindent \textit{Save this file as \texttt{my\_inventory\_questionnaire.docx} and edit your answers in place. Italic guidance lines can be left as-is.}

\vspace{0.2cm}

\hrule
\vspace{0.3cm}

# 1. Country and inventory year

\noindent\textbf{Country:} \dotfill\\
\textbf{Inventory year being prepared:} \dotfill

# 2. IPCC guidelines version

Tick one:

\begin{itemize}
  \item[$\square$] \textbf{IPCC 2006 Guidelines} (still the default reporting requirement in many countries)
  \item[$\square$] \textbf{IPCC 2019 Refinement} (newer; updated emission factors, more manure systems)
\end{itemize}

\noindent\textit{If unsure, ask your national focal point or pick 2006 — the app accepts both.}

# 3. Cattle sub-categories

\noindent\textit{List the sub-categories your inventory splits cattle into and the approximate head count of each. You can use any names — Claude will map them to the app's controlled vocabulary.}

\vspace{0.2cm}

\begin{tabularx}{\linewidth}{|X|X|c|c|}
\hline
\textbf{Sub-category name} & \textbf{Head count} & \textbf{Sex (M/F/mix)} & \textbf{Age class} \\ \hline
 & & & \\[10pt] \hline
 & & & \\[10pt] \hline
 & & & \\[10pt] \hline
 & & & \\[10pt] \hline
 & & & \\[10pt] \hline
\end{tabularx}

\vspace{0.2cm}

\noindent\textit{Example: dairy cows | 500,000 | F | adult \quad | \quad breeding bulls | 12,000 | M | adult}

# 4. Manure management

\noindent\textit{For each sub-category, roughly what \% of manure goes to each system? Per sub-category, the \% must sum to 100. Recognised systems:}

\begin{itemize}
  \setlength\itemsep{0pt}
  \item \texttt{pasture}, \texttt{daily\_spread}, \texttt{solid\_storage}, \texttt{solid\_storage\_covered}\textsuperscript{*}, \texttt{dry\_lot}, \texttt{deep\_bedding}
  \item \texttt{liquid\_slurry}, \texttt{lagoon}, \texttt{composting}, \texttt{anaerobic\_digester}\textsuperscript{*}, \texttt{aerobic\_treatment}\textsuperscript{*}, \texttt{burned\_for\_fuel}\textsuperscript{*}
\end{itemize}

\noindent\textit{\textsuperscript{*}available only when IPCC version = 2019 Refinement.}

\vspace{0.2cm}

\begin{tabularx}{\linewidth}{|X|X|X|X|X|}
\hline
\textbf{Sub-category} & \textbf{System 1 (\%)} & \textbf{System 2 (\%)} & \textbf{System 3 (\%)} & \textbf{System 4 (\%)} \\ \hline
 & & & & \\[10pt] \hline
 & & & & \\[10pt] \hline
 & & & & \\[10pt] \hline
\end{tabularx}

\vspace{0.2cm}

\noindent\textit{Example: dairy cows: pasture 40\%, solid\_storage 35\%, liquid\_slurry 15\%, lagoon 10\% (sums to 100).}

\newpage

# 5. Data fields you have

\noindent\textit{Tick the parameters for which you have country-specific values. Unticked parameters will be filled with IPCC defaults and flagged amber in the app's QA/QC tab for review.}

\vspace{0.2cm}

\noindent\textbf{Activity data (the population number)}\\
\begin{itemize}
  \setlength\itemsep{0pt}
  \item[$\square$] Animal population (head count) — \textbf{strongly recommended}
\end{itemize}

\noindent\textbf{Animal characteristics}\\
\begin{itemize}
  \setlength\itemsep{0pt}
  \item[$\square$] Body weight (kg) — \texttt{BW}
  \item[$\square$] Mature body weight (kg) — \texttt{MW}
  \item[$\square$] Daily weight gain (kg/day) — \texttt{WG}
  \item[$\square$] Fraction of females calving in the year — \texttt{pct\_calving}
\end{itemize}

\noindent\textbf{Production (dairy only)}\\
\begin{itemize}
  \setlength\itemsep{0pt}
  \item[$\square$] Daily milk yield per lactating cow (kg/day) — \texttt{Milk}
  \item[$\square$] Milk fat content (\%) — \texttt{Fat}
  \item[$\square$] Milk protein content (\%) — \texttt{MilkPR}
\end{itemize}

\noindent\textbf{Feed}\\
\begin{itemize}
  \setlength\itemsep{0pt}
  \item[$\square$] Digestible energy of feed (\% of gross energy) — \texttt{DE}
  \item[$\square$] Crude protein in feed (\%) — \texttt{CP}
\end{itemize}

\noindent\textbf{Climate (optional)}\\
\begin{itemize}
  \setlength\itemsep{0pt}
  \item[$\square$] Mean winter temperature (°C) — \texttt{Tw}
\end{itemize}

\noindent\textbf{IPCC equation coefficients} \textit{(rarely measured locally; leave unticked unless your country has a published value)}\\
\begin{itemize}
  \setlength\itemsep{0pt}
  \item[$\square$] \texttt{Cfi}, \texttt{Ca}, \texttt{C}, \texttt{Cp}, \texttt{Ym}, \texttt{Bo}, \texttt{ASH}, \texttt{UE}
  \item[$\square$] N\textsubscript{2}O emission factors (\texttt{EF3\_PRP}, \texttt{EF3\_S}, \texttt{EF4}, \texttt{EF5})
  \item[$\square$] Volatilisation / leaching fractions (\texttt{Frac\_GASMS}, \texttt{Frac\_LEACH\_H}, \texttt{Frac\_GASM\_PRP}, \texttt{Frac\_LEACH\_PRP})
\end{itemize}

# 6. Where do your uncertainty estimates come from?

\noindent Tick one (or combine if different sources for different parameters):

\begin{itemize}
  \setlength\itemsep{0pt}
  \item[$\square$] \textbf{(a) No uncertainty estimates} — use IPCC suggested $\pm\%$ from the catalogue
  \item[$\square$] \textbf{(b) Expert judgement $\pm\%$} — I'll write a single $\pm\%$ next to each value
  \item[$\square$] \textbf{(c) Measured confidence intervals} — I have explicit lower/upper bounds (or ± with sample size)
  \item[$\square$] \textbf{(d) A mix} — I'll specify per parameter
\end{itemize}

# 7. Time series (optional)

\begin{itemize}
  \item[$\square$] I have annual data for \textbf{5 or more years} that the app can use to estimate correlations between parameters.
\end{itemize}

\noindent\textbf{Earliest year:} \dotfill\quad \textbf{Latest year:} \dotfill

\noindent\textbf{Parameters covered (tick):}

\begin{itemize}
  \setlength\itemsep{0pt}
  \item[$\square$] N \quad $\square$ BW \quad $\square$ MW \quad $\square$ WG \quad $\square$ Milk
  \item[$\square$] Fat \quad $\square$ pct\_calving \quad $\square$ DE \quad $\square$ CP \quad $\square$ MilkPR
\end{itemize}

# 8. File(s) you'll upload

\noindent\textit{List the file names and a one-line description of what's in each.}

\vspace{0.3cm}
\noindent 1. \dotfill\\[6pt]
2. \dotfill\\[6pt]
3. \dotfill

# 9. Anything else you want Claude to know

\noindent\textit{Free-form note: anything unusual about your data, sub-category definitions specific to your country, or known quirks. Optional.}

\vspace{0.3cm}
\noindent\dotfill\\[8pt]
\dotfill\\[8pt]
\dotfill\\[8pt]
\dotfill

\vspace{0.5cm}

\begin{center}
\textcolor{cgiargreen}{\rule{14cm}{0.6pt}}\\[4pt]
\textbf{That's it.} Paste this filled page into the Translator and attach your files. Claude will take it from here.
\end{center}
